text.title: AI Pair-Coding Prompt Patterns Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns commonly observed in shipped ai-pair-coding-prompt-patterns work. Each pairs symptom + root-cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Prompt "add billing"; AI emits 800 LOC touching 12 files; nothing tests.
antipattern.root-cause: No pattern selected; no stop condition.
antipattern.fix: Pick a named pattern; force stop_conditions array; reject scaffolds without them. See rule named-pattern.

antipattern.symptom: AI keeps editing the same file in a loop trying to make a flaky test pass; 30 minutes of churn.
antipattern.root-cause: No explicit stop condition; AI uses token wall as stop.
antipattern.fix: Require stop_conditions with at least one numeric or boolean signal. See rule stop-condition-required.

antipattern.symptom: AI emits import foo from bar where bar.foo does not exist; CI breaks.
antipattern.root-cause: No repo-grep step; AI relied on training-data plausibility.
antipattern.fix: For tasks involving repo code, require pattern=repo-grep first or explicit repo_grep_targets. See rule repo-grep-before-invent.

antipattern.symptom: Prompt "add /pricing endpoint AND refactor billing module"; AI does both; diff is unreviewable.
antipattern.root-cause: Author treated AI session as cheap so bundled tasks.
antipattern.fix: Reject scaffolds with unit outside the enum or with task_summary mentioning >1 unit. See rule no-bundle.
